title: Address to the Federal Assembly
occasion: Annual Address to the Federal Assembly of the Russian Federation

paragraphs.text: Members of the Federal Assembly, citizens of Russia. This year's address comes at a time of enormous importance for our country. The challenges facing Russia are significant, but we have proven time and again that we are capable of meeting them. Today I will speak about our national priorities, our security, and our economic development.

Let me begin with the special military operation. Our servicemen and volunteers are displaying heroism and courage on the front lines. They are fighting for Russia, for our sovereignty, for our people. I want to address every one of them directly: the Motherland is proud of you. The entire country supports you.

We did not start this conflict. Let me remind you: it was NATO's relentless eastward expansion, the cultivation of neo-Nazi elements in Ukraine, the shelling of civilians in Donbas for eight years. All our attempts to resolve the situation peacefully were rebuffed. We had no choice but to act to protect our people and our security.

The West has launched an unprecedented sanctions campaign against Russia. They expected to destroy our economy within weeks. They predicted the collapse of the ruble, the shutdown of our industries, mass unemployment. What happened? Our economy proved far more resilient than they imagined. Russia's GDP grew while several European economies contracted.

Our defense industry has dramatically increased production. We are producing tanks, artillery systems, drones, and ammunition at rates that exceed what many Western nations can achieve. This is not because we want war. It is because we must be able to defend ourselves. And we will continue to strengthen our armed forces.

I want to say something very important about our strategic deterrent forces. Russia possesses the most advanced nuclear arsenal in the world. Our Sarmat intercontinental ballistic missile system, our Avangard hypersonic glide vehicles, our Kinzhal air-launched missiles. These are not just weapons. They are a guarantee that no one will ever be able to threaten Russia's existence.

Let me be clear to those in the West who speak of achieving a strategic defeat of Russia: such a scenario is impossible. Any attempt to intervene directly in the conflict, any threat to our territorial integrity, will be met with consequences that they have never faced in their history. This is not a bluff. They must understand this.

Now let me turn to our economy and social development. Despite the sanctions pressure, Russia's economy demonstrated remarkable adaptability. We have redirected trade flows. Our partnership with China has reached unprecedented levels. Trade with India, the Middle East, Africa, and Latin America is growing rapidly. The era of Western economic dominance is ending.

We must invest heavily in our technological sovereignty. Russia cannot depend on foreign technology that can be cut off at a moment's notice. I am announcing a new national program for technological development. We will invest in microelectronics, artificial intelligence, biotechnology, and advanced manufacturing. Our universities and research institutes will receive substantial new funding.

The demographic situation in Russia demands our most serious attention. We must support families and encourage higher birth rates. I am proposing an expansion of the maternity capital program and new tax benefits for families with multiple children. Housing programs for young families will be expanded. The future of Russia depends on the children being born today.

Infrastructure development must continue at an accelerated pace. New roads, railways, bridges, and ports. The development of the Northern Sea Route as a global logistics corridor. The modernization of our cities and rural areas alike. Every region of Russia, from Kaliningrad to Vladivostok, must feel the benefits of our national development.

Agriculture has become one of our greatest success stories. Russia is now one of the world's leading exporters of grain and agricultural products. Our farmers have achieved record harvests. We must continue to support agricultural development, expand food processing capacity, and ensure food security for every Russian citizen.

I want to speak about the men who have returned from the front and those who will return. They are the elite of our nation. They must receive every support: medical care, rehabilitation, education, housing, employment. A new state fund has been created specifically for veterans of the special military operation and their families.

The world is changing fundamentally. The unipolar model that the West tried to impose has failed. A new multipolar world order is emerging, and Russia is one of its sovereign centers. We will continue to develop partnerships within BRICS, the Shanghai Cooperation Organization, the Eurasian Economic Union. The future belongs to those who respect sovereignty and genuine international cooperation.

Let me address the question of negotiations. Russia has never refused to negotiate. But negotiations must be based on reality, not on the fantasies promoted by the Kyiv regime and its Western sponsors. The territorial realities on the ground must be acknowledged. Russia's security concerns must be addressed. NATO's expansion must be reversed.

To our citizens, I want to say: we are living through a time of testing. But Russia has faced such tests before and has always emerged stronger. Our unity, our patriotism, our willingness to stand together in defense of our homeland, these are the qualities that define us as a nation. Together, we will prevail. Russia's best days are ahead of us. Thank you.
